description_long: This object is a pair of over-ear protective earmuffs, commonly used for hearing protection in noisy urban environments such as construction sites or workshops. The earmuffs feature large, rounded red ear cups with black padding on the inner rims for comfort and sound isolation. The headband is thick, black, and appears to be padded or coated for additional comfort, with visible wear and white paint splatters indicating heavy use. Each ear cup is connected to the headband by a set of black metal or plastic arms, and there are visible adjustment mechanisms for fitting different head sizes. One side of the earmuffs has a prominent antenna-like rod, suggesting these may be electronic earmuffs with built-in radio or communication capabilities. The surface is mostly matte with some glossy worn spots, and there are labels and dials visible on the ear cups, indicating user controls.
description: A pair of red and black over-ear electronic earmuffs with visible wear, paint splatters, and adjustment arms. Features include a padded headband, antenna, and user controls on the ear cups.
description_view_0: Front angled view showing both red ear cups, black padded headband, and the antenna extending upward from the right side.
description_view_1: Side view focusing on the left ear cup, showing the adjustment arm, headband padding, and paint splatters.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the back of both ear cups, the antenna, and the adjustment arms.
description_view_3: Side view of the right ear cup, highlighting the antenna, a visible dial, and a label on the back.
